what: API quirks (these bite, write them down)
title: tceper — project notes for Claude
text: Host: https://sistemas.tcepe.tc.br/DadosAbertos/. previous host sistemas.tce.pe.gov.br retired returns empty bodies. Struts2 URLs: literal ! must survive request (e.g. Contratos!json). use httr2::req_url_path_append() – percent-encodes ! %21 server silently drops query params. Build URL paste0() httr2::request(). Response body: read httr2::resp_body_string(resp, encoding = "ISO-8859-1"). Query params: transcode UTF-8 -> Latin-1, mark bytes, percent-encode. See .tce_url_encode_latin1() R/request.R. Without , accented filters silently return 0 rows. Geo-restriction: API accepts requests Brazilian IPs. CRAN check machines, GitHub Actions runners, CI return TLS/connection errors. examples \dontrun{} API-touching vignette chunks use eval = FALSE, purl = FALSE (purl = FALSE critical – without , R CMD check extracts runs code regardless eval). Response limit: API caps 100,000 rows per request. package warns reached. Always narrow anoreferencia / mesreferencia / similar. Catalog offline: tce_catalog(), tce_endpoint(), tce_params(), tce_fields() read inst/extdata/tcepe_dados_abertos_catalogo.json work anywhere.

title: tceper — project notes for Claude
text: CURL_SSL_BACKEND = "secure-transport" macOS workaround (deleted R/zzz.R). new host modern TLS works stock LibreSSL. Non-ASCII characters R source. Use \uXXXX escapes need Unicode string literals; em-dashes/ellipses roxygen comments trigger portability WARNING. Verify tools::showNonASCIIfile().

title: Getting started with tceper
text: API returns 100,000 records per request. limit reached, tceper issues warning. work around , apply filters narrow query:
code: # Avoid unfiltered requests that may hit the 100k limit: # tce_municipal_expenditures()  # Choose a municipality code: library(dplyr) library(stringr)  tce_municipalities() |>   filter(str_detect(municipio, "Pesqueira")) |>   glimpse() # Rows: 1 # Columns: 5 # $ codigoibge        <chr> "2610905" # $ codigo            <chr> "P113" # $ unidadefederativa <chr> "PE" # $ municipio         <chr> "Pesqueira" # $ codigosagres      <chr> "5408"  # Filter by municipality and year: tce_municipal_expenditures(   codigo_municipio = "P113", # Pesqueira   ano_referencia   = "2025" )

title: Getting started with tceper
text: prefer call endpoint directly, use tce_request() pass API query names (shown tce_params()):
code: tce_request("Contratos", CodigoEfiscoUG = "510101", AnoContrato = "2025")

title: Getting started with tceper
text: wrapper functions cache results memory. default TTL 1 hour. can change TTL globally:
code: tce_contracts(codigo_efisco_ug = "510101")  # hits the API tce_contracts(codigo_efisco_ug = "510101")  # instant (from cache)  # Bypass cache tce_contracts(codigo_efisco_ug = "510101", cache = FALSE)  # Inspect and manage tce_cache_info() tce_cache_clear() options(tceper.cache_ttl = 7200)  # 2 hours

title: Perform a request to the TCE-PE Open Data API — tce_request
text: Low-level function sends GET request TCE-PE Open Data API returns response content tibble. Higher-level wrapper functions tce_contracts(), tce_state_expenditures(), etc. call function internally. can use tce_request() directly access API endpoint, including yet dedicated wrapper.

title: Perform a request to the TCE-PE Open Data API — tce_request
code: tce_request(   endpoint,   ...,   clean_names = TRUE,   max_tries = 3,   progress = getOption("tceper.progress", TRUE),   verbose = getOption("tceper.verbose", FALSE) )

title: Perform a request to the TCE-PE Open Data API — tce_request
text: endpoint character string API method name (e.g., "Contratos", "DespesasEstaduais"). See https://sistemas.tcepe.tc.br/DadosAbertos/Exemplo!listar full list available endpoints. ... Named query parameters. can use either API names (CamelCase/UPPERCASE) snake_case aliases shown tce_params(). NULL values silently dropped. clean_names TRUE (default), column names converted snake_case via janitor::clean_names(). Set FALSE keep original column names returned API. max_tries Maximum number retry attempts failed requests (default: 3). Passed httr2::req_retry(). progress TRUE, displays progress messages via cli. Defaults value getOption("tceper.progress"), TRUE unset. verbose TRUE, prints final API URL, parameters, response metadata (status, content-type, size, elapsed time). Defaults getOption("tceper.verbose", FALSE).

title: Perform a request to the TCE-PE Open Data API — tce_request
text: tibble response content. Returns zero-row tibble records found.

title: Perform a request to the TCE-PE Open Data API — tce_request
text: https://sistemas.tcepe.tc.br/DadosAbertos/

title: Perform a request to the TCE-PE Open Data API — tce_request
text: API host accepts connections Brazilian IP addresses. Calls outside Brazil dropped network level surface connection TLS error. offline discovery functions (tce_catalog(), tce_params(), tce_fields()) require network access work anywhere.

title: Perform a request to the TCE-PE Open Data API — tce_request
text: TCE-PE API uses Struts2-style URLs ! invokes method (e.g. Contratos!json). URL built paste0() preserve literal ! – using httr2::req_url_path_append() encode %21, causing server ignore query parameters.

title: Perform a request to the TCE-PE Open Data API — tce_request
text: API returns 100,000 records per request. limit reached, warning issued suggesting use additional filters.

title: Perform a request to the TCE-PE Open Data API — tce_request
text: TCE-PE backend runs ISO-8859-1 (Latin-1), UTF-8. package handles transparently directions: Response body decoded Latin-1 returned UTF-8 R strings, accented Portuguese characters come cleanly (São, Município, Conceição, etc.). Query parameters transcoded UTF-8 -> Latin-1 percent-encoding, filtering accented values works expected (e.g. tce_municipalities(municipio = "São José da Coroa Grande")). can pass R string accents – conversion automatic.

title: Perform a request to the TCE-PE Open Data API — tce_request
text: tceper.progress – set FALSE suppress progress messages. tceper.verbose – set TRUE always print request/response details. tceper.cache_ttl – cache time--live seconds (default: 3600).

title: Perform a request to the TCE-PE Open Data API — tce_request
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Not run: these examples reach the live TCE-PE API, which only accepts # connections from Brazilian IP addresses and is therefore unreachable # from CRAN check machines and most CI runners.  # Direct query to any endpoint tce_request("Contratos", CodigoEfiscoUG = "510101")  # Show full request/response details tce_request("Contratos", CodigoEfiscoUG = "510101", verbose = TRUE)  # Keep original column names tce_request("Municipios", clean_names = FALSE) } # }

title: tceper: Access the Open Data API of TCE-PE — tceper-package
text: R interface Open Data API Tribunal de Contas Estado de Pernambuco (TCE-PE), Court Accounts State Pernambuco, Brazil. Provides tidy, ready--use functions query public data revenues, expenditures, commitments, procurement, contracts, agreements, public works, legal processes, personnel reference tables state municipal government entities Pernambuco.

title: tceper: Access the Open Data API of TCE-PE — tceper-package
text: TCE-PE API host (sistemas.tcepe.tc.br) geo-restricted accepts requests Brazilian IP addresses. Users outside Brazil see connection TLS errors. discovery functions (tce_catalog(), tce_endpoint(), tce_params(), tce_fields()) unaffected – read built-catalog work offline location.

title: tceper: Access the Open Data API of TCE-PE — tceper-package
text: Discover endpoints tce_catalog() (filter search =). Inspect input parameters tce_params() output fields tce_fields(). Query using wrapper function (e.g. tce_contracts()) low-level tce_request().

title: tceper 0.1.4
text: Quoted software API names (‘Open Data API’, ‘httr2’, ‘cli’, ‘snake_case’) DESCRIPTION Title Description, added web reference API Description. Unwrapped offline-runnable examples (tce_catalog(), tce_endpoint(), tce_params(), tce_fields(), tce_cache_clear(), tce_cache_info()) run automatic checks. Added comment remaining \dontrun{} examples explaining reach live, Brazil-geofenced API run CRAN.

title: tceper 0.1.3
text: Migrated new API host sistemas.tcepe.tc.br. previous host sistemas.tce.pe.gov.br retired TCE-PE now returns empty responses. built-endpoints catalog updated.

title: tceper 0.1.3
text: Latin-1 query encoding. TCE-PE backend matches values ISO-8859-1 bytes, accented Portuguese inputs previously returned zero rows. tce_request() now transcodes UTF-8 inputs Latin-1 percent-encodes byte--byte. tce_municipalities(municipio = "São José da Coroa Grande") now matches expected. Verbose mode rewritten. verbose = TRUE now prints structured block endpoint, full URL, parameters list (long values truncated), response: HTTP status, content-type, body size, elapsed time. Cache hit log. Cached results now report age row count (Cache hit (12s old, 1334 rows).) tceper.progress .

title: tceper 0.1.3
text: Friendlier errors. HTTP, JSON-parse, API-status, DNS, TLS, timeout failures now produce structured cli::cli_abort() messages URL, response body excerpt (applicable), actionable next-step hint. API’s mensagem field surfaced present. Empty results explain . tamanhoResultado 0 warning suggests relaxing removing filters. 100k record limit warning now spells rows likely missing suggests common narrowing filters.

title: tceper 0.1.3
text: New ## Network access rewritten ## Encoding sections ?tce_request cover geo-restriction (Brazilian IPs ) Latin-1 round-trip. Vignettes carry callout noting requests require Brazilian IP, use purl = FALSE R CMD check try extract run API-bound chunks. cran-comments.md documents geo-restriction (CRAN policy 1.1) encoding handling CRAN reviewer. inst/WORDLIST added spelling::spell_check_package(). _pkgdown.yml URL corrected (placeholder BigDataPE -> tceper); site rebuilt new content.

title: tceper 0.1.2
text: Removed duplicate .tce_catalog() definition. function defined catalog.R (-memory caching) introspection.R (without caching). uncached version introspection.R loaded second silently overrode cached version, causing JSON catalog re-parsed every call. Now cached version catalog.R used. Fixed double .tce_map_params() call tce_cache_key(). Parameter names mapped twice, harmless wasteful.

title: tceper 0.1.2
text: Rewrote README.md complete sections: quick start, catalog exploration, verbose mode, cache, parameter validation, options table, API limits. Improved tceper-package.R full package-level documentation including workflow, parameter names, options. Fixed vignette getting-started: corrected record limit 10,000 100,000 typo “municapality” → “municipality”. Updated _pkgdown.yml: added print methods tceper-package reference index. Fixed DESCRIPTION: replaced placeholder pkgdown URL actual site URL. Updated NEWS.md reflect current API (parameter names without leading dots, translated parameter names).

title: tceper 0.1.1
text: Fixed critical URL encoding bug: httr2::req_url_path_append() percent-encoding ! character Struts2-style URLs (Contratos!json → Contratos%21json), causing API server ignore query parameters return unfiltered results. URL now constructed directly via paste0() passed httr2::request(), preserving literal ! separator (#1).

title: tceper 0.1.1
text: -memory cache 71 wrapper functions parameter-aware cache keys. unique combination endpoint + parameters gets cache entry. example, tce_contracts(codigo_efisco_ug = "510101") tce_contracts(codigo_efisco_ug = "510102") cached separately. TTL defaults 1 hour; configurable via options(tceper.cache_ttl = <seconds>). tce_cache_clear() — clear pattern-matched cached entries. tce_cache_info() — inspect current cache contents age. functions accept cache = FALSE force fresh request. verbose parameter functions. TRUE, prints final API URL (query parameters) making request. Also shows helper commands tce_params() tce_fields(). Can set globally via options(tceper.verbose = TRUE). Endpoint catalog introspection. New functions discovering API without leaving R: tce_catalog() — list endpoints search support. tce_endpoint() — get metadata single endpoint. tce_params() — list input parameters api_name r_name. tce_fields() — list output fields. Parameter validation mapping. Query parameters validated catalog. Invalid parameter names trigger helpful error listing allowed parameters. Users can pass snake_case names (e.g. codigo_efisco_ug) automatically mapped API’s expected names (e.g. CodigoEfiscoUG).
